title: The Will The Cause Of Woe.
content:
When man is punish'd, he is plagued still,
    Not for the fault of nature, but of will.
meaning:
Robert Herrick's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "The Will The Cause Of Woe."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
